title: Sangīt Manjarī
text:
Sangīt Manjarī




Preserving India’s musical heritage means balancing deep respect for tradition with thoughtful documentation. Much of Indian classical music has been passed down orally through the guru-shishya system, creating profound, personal learning—but also making the tradition vulnerable to loss without written records. Saving old bandishes is not just archival work; it’s about keeping the voices of past generations alive for today and tomorrow.
Saṅgīt Manjarī is a key step in this endeavor. Compiled by Pt. Ramprasanna Bandyopadhyay of the Bishnupur Gharānā and first published in 1907, it is one of the earliest written collections of a gharānā’s music, drawing on both his father’s teaching and the broader Bishnupur tradition. The collection is remarkable for its range: while Dhrupad is central, it also features Khayāl, Tappā, Thumri, Chaturang, Jhulan, Trivat, and Thillānā—few works from that era showcase such diversity. Later, around 1935, Pt. Ramprasanna’s brother, Pt. Gopeswar Bandyopadhyay, expanded and edited the book, helping it serve as a core resource for musicians and scholars.
Despite its importance, Saṅgīt Manjarī has long been difficult to access. Scanned versions online are in Braj Bhasha, written with Bengali script, making them hard for most modern readers. Guided by my teacher, Guruji Pt. Arijit Mahalanabis—whose deep knowledge and pioneering work on reviving rare musical texts inspired this project—our team created a new edition (now available on Amazon). This version transliterates the original Braj into Roman script, corrects minor errors, and presents the music using Bhatkhande notation widely adopted in Indian classical music. Our goal is to stay true to the original while making it relevant and accessible today.
This website is the companion to that book. Here, you will find a digital selection of compositions from Saṅgīt Manjarī. Rather than simply reproduce the original, our aim is to make these materials practical and inspiring for the next generation.
Because Saṅgīt Manjarī is so extensive, we are releasing material in installments. The first set features compositions that influenced our national poet Viswakavi Rabindranath Tagore, highlighting the Bishnupur Gharānā’s unique impact on his music and, more broadly, on modern Bengali music.
Ultimately, we hope this project will help preserve India’s musical legacy by combining careful editing with digital sharing. We invite others working with different gharānās to join in and help revive more rare musical traditions.



  
  
    
  
  
  
    Sangīt Manjarī: Tagore Edition
    By Ramprasanna Banerjee, Dr. Suparno Bhattacharya, Pt. Arijit Mahalanabis
    
    
      View on Amazon

title: Rāg Suraṭ
text: Composer: Mahārāja navalakiśor  ·  Tāl: Cautāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Suraṭ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

ye batiyā mere cita caḍhi nisa dina kālī nāma raṭata rahemo rasanā kahā karuṅ mana na thira rahata neka

Antarā

kāma krodha lobha moha mada matsara ahaṅkāraye ati durnibāra māno matta gajendra eka te eka

Sañcārī

jāko vaśa karaṇako bhakti tuva mahāvatajñāna aṅkuśa sākāra viveka

Ābhog

navalakiśora oura kāso binati karajāko tua caraṇa śaraṇako ṭeka








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle. In this layout, it is divided into blocks corresponding to 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Bāgīśvarī
text: Composer: Niyāmat khā  ·  Tāl: Tilwādā (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Bāgīśvarī
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

tuma bina kaise rahoṅgī re piyu

Antarā

ye niśa dina mohe kaṭhina bītata hai
vyākula bhaye jiya morā re








NoteReading the notation





Tilwādā(Madhyalay) is a 16-beat cycle. In this Madhyalay composition, the notation is visually divided as 2 + 4 + 4 + 4 + 2 to match the phrasing. Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 9, and 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Basant
text: Composer: Anantalāl  ·  Tāl: Jhāpatāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Basant
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

sarasa sundara bara basanta ritu āyejagata jana kī mana me caina sukha chāye

Antarā

sakala bana upavana praphulla phula sājekuñja kānana kusuma para bhavara rāje

Sañcārī

matta kokila madhura śākhā para gāvedaśa diśa sugandha śītala pavana dhāve

Ābhog

aise riturāja saṃsāra sukhadāiraina-dina sakala birahini-gaṇa satāi








NoteReading the notation





Jhāpatāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, tali 2 at beat 3, khālī O at beat 6, and tali 3 at beat 8.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Iman kalyāṇ
text: Composer: Budhaprakāś  ·  Tāl: Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Iman kalyāṇ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

śankara śiva pinākī gaṅgādharavikhadhara vāmadeva īśvara ḍamarū kara

Antarā

bhasma anga śobhita bhujanga bhālacandraśṛngī phukata hai bholā digambara

Sañcārī

tilaka lalāṭa gale ruṇḍhamāla trinayanabara dātā gaurī sanga trisula dharana

Ābhog

paśūpatī viśvanātha mṛtyunjayajapa mahādeva nāma hara hara








NoteReading the notation





Suraphāktāl is a 10-beat cycle. In this layout, it is divided into blocks corresponding to 4 + 2 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Chāyānaṭ
text: Composer: Raṅganāth  ·  Tāl: Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Chāyānaṭ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

śambhū hara maheśa ādi trilocana bhava-bhaya-hara bhaveśa
dīnanātha dānava-dalana dīneśvara

Antarā

jaṭājūta pinākī bhasma runḍamala garala gare dhara
hara oḍha bāghāmbara

Sanchārī

nācata candra bhāla bam bam bāje ghana ghana
ati apūrva hari guṇa gāvata tripureśvara

Ābhog

bīracandra narapati prakāśa karana so
adhara dhara sumadhura tāna sāca sundara








NoteReading the notation





Suraphāktāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 4 + 2 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, and 3 at beat 7.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Bhairav
text: Composer: Mānadās  ·  Tāl: Āḍācautāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Bhairav
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

rudradeva trinayana aruṇa jaṭājūṭa
pināka dhāraṇa śivahara girijā pati bṛkhaba bāhana

Antarā

nīlakaṇṭha gaja-carmāmvara ḍamaru bāje bājana tāpara
triśūlapāṇī purāṇa purukha sakala dīna trāṇa

Sañcārī

nara kaṅkāla māla phaṇigaṇa phana phanāta
aradhacandra bhāla bhasma aṅga bhūkhana

Ābhog

yogīsvara maheśa jaga dagdha karata nāśa
mānadāsa unake paga nitahī karata dhyana








NoteReading the notation





Āḍācautāl is a 14-beat cycle. In this notation layout, the fourteen columns are visually grouped as 2 + 4 + 4 + 4, so the divider lines fall after beats 2, 6, and 10.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Rāmkalī
text: Composer: Acapala  ·  Tāl: Tintāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Rāmkalī
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

pyālā mujhe bhari de re matavāri morābīta gailī sagarī raina bhailī bhora

Antarā

hama tuma pive chakāi chakāvedurajana loga dekha jare karahi śora








NoteReading the notation





Tintāl is a 16-beat cycle divided as 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, tali 2 at beat 5, khālī O at beat 9, and tali 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. title: Rāg Bhūpāli
text: Composer: Jānakī dās  ·  Tāl: Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Bhūpāli
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

pracaṇḍa garjana sajala barakhā ritukāma āgama ata birahinī jiyana tarjana

Antarā

jhaṭa asa dāminī mataṅga sama yāminīarū druma cāpa karkaśa būnda bhārī barakhana

Sañcārī

cātaka cakora piu piu karata soramaura bikaṭa borī catura diśana

Ābhog

kadamba taru kusumita subāsī vṛndāvanatiyā iyā iyā iyā iyā iyāgāvata brajavāsī harakha mana








NoteReading the notation





Suraphāktāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 4 + 2 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, tali 2 at beat 5, and tali 3 at beat 7.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Bāhār
text: Tāl: Cautāl (Drut)

title: Rāg Bāhār
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

phuli bana ghana more, āye basanta riaba bahata pavana manda manda samīraṇa mana bhāve,jaba madhup bṛnda nirata kara guṇjārnayī nayī kaliyan para jāye, cuyā kaharata

Antarā

ketakī gulāb aura campā bakula belā, ati komal dala kusum sahita praphullita bhaināth nāth nirata karata nārī nirakha natha








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, khālī O at beat 3, tali 2 at beat 5, khālī O at beat 7, tali 3 at beat 9, and tali 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Bhairavī
text: Composer: Raṅganāth  ·  Tāl: Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Bhairavī
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

nāda parama vidyā dehi bhavānī  nisa dina mange tose rāga tāla bāṇī

Antarā

suta raṅganātha tujha caraṇa jugala dhyānī  gāve bajāve jaya jaya śiva rāṇī

Sañcārī

āgama nigama gaṇeśa nārada śūlapāṇi  bhṛṅgī ādi muni jana pūje joḍa pāṇi

Ābhog

ita nāhin anta kahi na pāve bhavānī  apani ye jasa kṛpā kīje muḍhe karo jñānī








NoteReading the notation





Suraphāktāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 4 + 2 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, tali 2 at beat 5, and tali 3 at beat 7.

Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

text: Composer: Jānakī dās  ·  Tāl: Jhāpatāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Sindhu
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

muralī dhuni suni alī mai yamunā tīratabaso hama tana mana jobana so bikāi

Antarā

pavana gati hīna bhai yamunā ujāna bahebisara gai gavana śuka sāri hamārī pyārī

Sañcārī

thagita bhai mīna gau tṛṇa na cabāve punabacharā na piye stana aisī sunāi

Ābhog

chaila gara ḍori lagābe brajanārījala bharana bhula gai ṭhāḍi sakhiri roi








NoteReading the notation





Jhāpatāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, tali 2 at beat 3, khālī O at beat 6, and tali 3 at beat 8.

title: Rāg Behāg
text: Composer: Suratasen  ·  Tāl: Jhāpatāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Behāg
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

mere danda dala sāje daśaratha-suta rāma,
padama daśa koṭita koṭi kopa dhāyo

Antarā

lagana cahu ora vikala ghana haka de
banka gaḍa laṅka ko caḍhi dabāyo

Sanchārī

ghananāda rāvaṇa aura kumbhakarana
palaka saṃhāra kara, avadha me sītā lāyo

Ābhog

nagara se loga saba sumana barakhā vana
nirakhata suratasena ānanda pāyo








NoteReading the notation





Jhāpatāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 3, O (khālī) at beat 6, and 3 at beat 8.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Tilak kāmod
text: Composer: Raṅganāth  ·  Tāl: Jhāpatāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Tilak kāmod
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

kauna rūpa bane ho rājādhirāja
āju naina nirakhi raṅganātha gāve

Antarā

taji aragajā candana, bibhūtī aṅga bhūkhana,
jaṭā mukuṭa kaisī bani āve

Sanchārī

kaiso mukhamaṇḍala, jhalasa śruti kuṇḍala,
bhai candra bhāla mṛga chāla pāve

Ābhog

baraji bara ambara, pahana bāghāmbara
śīṣa para gaṅgādhara dharasi dhāve

title: Rāg Śukla belāvalī
text: Composer: Nāyak baiju  ·  Tāl: Jhāpatāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Śukla belāvalī
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

jñāna raṅga dhyāna raṅga raṅga aura vijñāna
raṅga mana raṅga saba aṅgana raṅga raṅga

Antarā

prathama rāmakṛṣṇa raṅga rahima karima raṅga
ghaṭa ghaṭa brahma raṅga vyoma vyoma tanu raṅga
hara raṅga raṅga

Sanchārī

japa raṅga tapa raṅga tīratha brata nema raṅga
sarvamayī karama dharma jaga raṅga raṅga

Ābhog

jīva jantu pannaga paśu eka īśvara
raṅga raṅga sura nara muni saṅga
baiju prabhu kṛṣṇa raṅga raṅga

title: Rāg Gauḍ malhār
text: Composer: Tānasen  ·  Tāl: Cautāl (Vilambit)

title: Rāg Gauḍ malhār
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

indrahunkī asabārī papīyana kī batiya
deśa-deśa khabara kārī

Antarā

garaje damāmā bāje dhuravā niśāna bāṇa
bādara kī phauja caḍhi bundahu ki tīra māri

Sanchārī

dāmini sohi rañjaka topa golā bāṇa chuṭe
kākara jiye birahinī bicārī

Ābhog

kahe miyan tānasena jinake piyā bideśa
tinako ye jaṅga bhārī








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Niśāsāg
text: Composer: Tulasīdās  ·  Tāl: Jhāpatāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Niśāsāg
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

dusaha dokha-dukha dalani, karu devi dayā

Antarā-1

viśva mūlāhasi jana sānukulāsi
karaśūla dhāriṇi mahāmūla māyā

Antarā-2

taḍita garbhāṅga sarvāṅga sundara lasita
divya paṭa bhavya bhūkhana birāje

Antarā-3

vāla mṛga mañju khañjani vilocani
candravadani lakhi koṭa rati māra lāje

Antarā-4

rūpa-sukha-śīla sīmāhsi bhīmāhasi
rāmhāsi vāmāsi vara vuddhi vāṇī

Antarā-5

chamukha heramva amvāsi jagadamvike
śamvu jāyāsi jaya jaya bhavāni

Antarā-6

caṇḍa bhuja daṇḍa khaṇḍani vihaṇḍani muṇḍa
mahikha mada bhaṅga kari aṅga tore

Antarā-7

śumbha niśumva kumbhīśa raṇa keśarina
krodha vāridha vairī vṛnda vore

Antarā-8

nigama āgama agama gurviva tava guṇa kathana
urvivadhara bhaṇata dohe sahasa jihvā

Antarā-9

dehi mā mohe praṇa prema nita nema yaha
rāma ghana śyāma tulasī papīhā








NoteReading the notation





Jhāpatāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 3, O (khālī) at beat 6, and 3 at beat 8.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Ālāhiyā
text: Composer: Adāraṅga  ·  Tāl: Dhamār (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Ālāhiyā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

ḍapha bājata mohana chabi chājata
nācata gata so ānanda de de de de tāri

Antarā

tāla mṛdaṅga upāṅga bīṇa dhuna
nevara kī jhanakāra kārī

Sanchārī

gvāla bāla saba saṅga sakhā ṭhāḍhe
bane sāgara nāgara banacārī

Ābhog

dekhi adāraṅga bhūla pyārī ko
aṅga samāna tāri








NoteReading the notation





Dhamār (Madhyalay) is a 14-beat cycle, visually divided here as 3 + 2 + 2 + 3 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the fourteen columns correspond to beats 1–14. Sam X is at beat 1, followed by an unmarked sub-division at beat 4, then 2 at beat 6, O (khālī) at beat 8, and 3 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

text: Composer: Hitaparakh  ·  Tāl: Cautāl (Drut)

title: Rāg Hamīr
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

bunda pavana puravāi garaja garaja barakhata ghana

Antarā

umaḍa ghumaḍa cahudisa ghana ati ghera āvata,
madana dahana karata tana, una bina kaise rahata bhavana








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Āḍānā
text: Composer: Kṣemarasik  ·  Tāl: Cautāl (Drut)

title: Rāg Āḍānā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

beṇī nirakhata bhujaṅga patāla loka gayo,
dṛga dekhata mīna jala madha chipata rī

Antarā

mukha dekha kalā hīna uḍapata uḍa vyoma basata
hasata daśana dūta nirakhata hata anāra darakha marori

Sanchārī

kuca dekha cakravāka nagara na rahe yaha kāraṇa
tana duti dekha kāñcana āgame jarorī

Ābhog

siṃha bākī kaṭi nehāra gayo vana para darapa hāra,
kṣemarasika chaba nirakhata deta balihārī








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Suraṭ
text: Composer: Chavināyak  ·  Tāl: Tintāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Suraṭ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

bāja rahī sakhiyā re muraliyā tana mana sura nāma dhāma dhara
jāgī nanadī ḍara pāi āli aba

Antarā

bakhata na jāna niṭhura muraliyā āju gayo sakhi prāṇa ,
dharama karama gayo pītama kāraṇa āju rākho mero māna mohana aba








NoteReading the notation





Tintāl is a 16-beat cycle divided as 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 9, and 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Nāyakī kānaḍā
text: Composer: Ānandaghana  ·  Tāl: Dhamār (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Nāyakī kānaḍā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

āyo phāguna baḍho māna
nanda ko chaila ḍole gokula ki ḍagara

Antarā

baḍho bhāga anurāga yā braja ko
bājata jhānjha ḍapha nanda kī bagara

Sanchārī

raṅga picakārī sabana pai ḍārata
sudha na rahe kachu aba ghara ghara

Ābhog

ānanda umangī calo braja māhi
gvāla bāla saṅga kara dhara dhara








NoteReading the notation





Dhamār is a 14-beat cycle divided as 5 + 2 + 3 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the fourteen columns correspond to beats 1–14, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 6, O (khālī) at beat 8, and 3 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Behāg
text: Composer: Adāraṅga  ·  Tāl: Dhamār (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Behāg
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

āju raṅga khelata hori baraja me dhuma maco ri

Antarā

śyāmasundara braja nāgara ke rasa dekhata guruyana corī

Sañcārī

gāve bajāve rasa upajāve raṅga bhara kañcana kī picakārī

Ābhog

cuvā candana aura aragajā abīra liye barajorī








NoteReading the notation





Dhamār tāl is a 14-beat cycle divided as 5 + 2 + 3 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the fourteen columns correspond to beats 1–14, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 6, O (khālī) at beat 8, and 3 at beat 11.

title: Rāg Darabāri kānaḍā
text: Composer: Tānasen  ·  Tāl: Cautāl (Vilambit)

title: Rāg Darabāri kānaḍā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

acala birāja ho siṃhāsana baiṭhe
rājādhirāja rājārāma pṛthvī para

Antarā

takhata bakhata tumhi dino karatāra
nistāra karana ko, ānanda bhayo taha pura ghara ghara

Sanchārī

aneka aneka bhātanako naga naga takhata śobhā deta
māno indu jyota jhala mala kara

Ābhog

tānasena tua guṇa kaha na sakata
astuta karata saba loga aura tuhi jahāna para-eka nara








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Āḍānā
text: Composer: Manaraṅga  ·  Tāl: Tintāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Āḍānā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

aba mori pāyelā bājanu solālana tihāre darasa hama kaise āu

Antarā

jāgata hai mori nanadī jeṭhaniyāāvana ke samaya na pāu








NoteReading the notation





Tintāl is a 16-beat cycle divided as 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 9, and 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Bahār
text: Composer: Raṅganātha  ·  Tāl: Tīvrā (Drut)

title: Rāg Bahār
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

āju bahata sugandha pavana sumanda madhura basanta me
hara makura para yutha madhupa madahara nirata kara rava kuñja me

Antarā

kahi koyaliyā kuhu karahi āmuvā ke ḍāra raṅga me
kahi beli cāmeli gulāba gendā campa raṅga biraṅga me

Sanchārī

ita jobana madamātī yuvati jvali rahī bina kāntame
pūkāra ghana hā nātha nātha vihata bhai prāṇāntame

Ābhog

suni śravaṇa rava raṅganātha kahata bacāve nātha kusaṅga me
ehi raṅga ḍhaṅga anaṅga mada so utārī rākha susaṅga me








NoteReading the notation





Tīvrā tāl is a 7-beat cycle divided as 3 + 2 + 2. Each row below shows two consecutive cycles side-by-side: the first seven columns form one cycle (sam X at beat 1, vibhāgs 2 and 3 marking beats 4 and 6), and the next seven columns form the following cycle.

title: Rāg Bāhār
text: Tāl: Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Bāhār
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

āye ritu pati vasanta rāja,sakala dala sājana kara bolekoyeliyā rava sunata, śravaṅa kuhu ravamomana dara pāve

Antarā

praphullita saba vana ghanacampā bakula belari sabaati sugandha bahata pavanamanda manda ali kulavyākula bhaye








NoteReading the notation





Suraphāktāl(Madhyalay) is a 10-beat cycle. In this notation, it is divided as 4 + 2 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, and 3 at beat 7.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Rāmkali
text: Composer: Jñānaraṅga  ·  Tāl: Jhāpatāl (Drut)

title: Rāg Rāmkali
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

bājata bīna pravīna rabāba kinnara
gāvata indra darabāra

Antarā

ughaṭata madhura dhuna cahudisa chāyo
aura tāna mṛdaṅga dhuṅkāra

Sanchārī

siṃhāsana para baiṭhe surapati
nirakha koṭi mahajyota hāra

Ābhog

yaha sabhā ko sāja dekhe saba
mana me ānanda pāve apāra








NoteReading the notation





Jhāpatāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 3, O (khālī) at beat 6, and 3 at beat 8.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Iman kalyāṅ
text: Composer: Manaraṅga  ·  Tāl: Ektāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Iman kalyāṅ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

baṅgari mori murakai jina chuo laṅgaravā
kara ke kalāi ḍarana lāgī nibhara ḍara jeṭhānī deta gārī

Antarā

saṅga ke lugāi saba dūra mura musakāya gai
kara ke pharaka erī








NoteReading the notation





Ektāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Pūrvī
text: Composer: Kṛṣṇarasik  ·  Tāl: Dhamār (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Pūrvī
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

bīna bajāya re mana le gayo

Antarā

madhura madhura dhuna adharana dhara re
rasa bhari tāna sunāya re mana le gayo








NoteReading the notation

The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Iman kalyaṅ
text: Composer: Sadāraṅga  ·  Tāl: Jaladtetālā (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Iman kalyaṅ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

caturaṅga rasa sana gāye ho gāyana guṇī āye
mahammadaśā ke ghara kāja hastī turaṅga sarasa sukha pāve
dāna māna sundara jara śira pāiye

Antarā

sare sare gaga regagare sa papa gaga resa sare gama papa
dhadhapa dhadhapa ninidha ninidhadha papamama gagarega garesa

Sanchārī

nāderder dāni tomderder diyānāre tom tānānatāna
tānānānā nāder der dāni tadāni tadāre tānānānā nānā
dīm tānādīm tānānānā nānānānā nānānānā nānānānā
dhalāṃ dhalāṃ dhalāṃ dhalāṃ

Ābhog

dṛgitoma dṛgitoma dṛmikeṭe dṛmikeṭe thārikeṭe
thārikeṭe tākujhāku tāku jhāku nāga diga diga
thaṃ naṃ naṃ thuṃ diga diga toma ambo ambo amboiyā
tiyā iyā tiya iyā tiyā iyā tiyā iyā tiyā iyā

title: Rāg Bṛndāvanī sāraṅg
text: Composer: Raṅganāth  ·  Tāl: Tīvrā (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Bṛndāvanī sāraṅg
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

jaya prabala begavati sureśvari jayati jaya gaṅge
trijagata tāriṇi jaga kaluṣa nāśini pāravati
raṅganātha suta para neka karahara tapana suta bhara antime

Antarā

tua nīra niramala karata ḍhala ḍhala tīra taṭa ati śobhini
naga nandini itha makara dinakara candrimāghame dehi pada yuga bhāgame








NoteReading the notation





Tīvrā is a 7-beat cycle divided as 3 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows two complete cycles: the fourteen columns correspond to beats 1–7 of the first cycle and 1–7 of the second cycle, with sam X at beats 1 and 8, 2 at beats 4 and 11, and 3 at beats 6 and 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Darabāri toḍī
text: Composer: Adāraṅga  ·  Tāl: Tintāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Darabāri toḍī
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

kāhna na kara mose rāra aba hi jāya karihū pukāra
laṅgara niḍara gagariyā dihnī ḍāla re

Antarā

ācara pakara jāya yaśodā so kahihū
apana ḍhīṭa laṅgara kahi samhāla re








NoteReading the notation





Tintāl is a 16-beat cycle divided as 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 9, and 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Bahār
text: Composer: Tānarāj  ·  Tāl: Cautāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Bahār
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

manamatha tana dahe mero dukhata, sakala druma nai bāhāra pāyeri
hasata bhramara koyalā deta gāri

Antarā

eka sugandha bahata pavana, hā hā karata birahi jana,
tānarāja gāvata bana bana raci eri pyāri








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Naṭ malhār
text: Composer: Niyāmat khān  ·  Tāl: Ektāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Naṭ malhār
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

mori nai lagana lāgīre piyā tuma sana morāsundara sneha lagī ghari pala china dina

Antarā

jīyakī sakuca tyāji piyā so milana kīne kauna sirāta gāta bāra dai tana mana dhana








NoteReading the notation





Ektāl is a 12-beat cycle divided into six vibhāgs of two beats each (2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12. Following the manuscript, the vibhāgs are marked as X (sam, beat 1), O (khālī, beat 3), X (typically tali 2, beat 5), O (khālī, beat 7), 3 (tali, beat 9), and 4 (tali, beat 11).
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

text: Composer: Haridāsa svāmī  ·  Tāl: Tīvrā (Drut)

title: Rāg Bhīmapalaśrī
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

nācata tribhaṅga ye nanda nandana vṛndāvana yamunātaṭaamita manamatha madavimardāna mṛdula abhinava jalada sundara aṅga

Antarā-1

tana dipata dāminī dūrakārī mukha sudhākara manahārīkuṭila-dṛṣṭa kaṭākṣa-saṃyuta capala nayana kuraṅga

Antarā-2

karata hāsa vilāsa bhāvana parasapara mukha adhara cumbanagāḍha parirambhanāi china china uṭhata adabhuta raṅga

Antarā-3

paṭatāla mañjira banśī madhura vīṇa rabāba ḍhakkādapha uḍata upaṅga chalaharī khañjarī mṛdaṅga

Antarā-4

tak tak tak dig dig dig dig dṛmika dṛmikaṭachaṃ naṃ naṃ naṃ naṃ naṃ naṃ dhṛdhika dhṛdhikaṭachaṃ naṃ naṃ naṃ naṃ naṃ naṃ tā tā thei tā tā theidhā dhā dhā dhu dhu dhu dhu dhuṅga








NoteReading the notation





Tīvrā tāl is a 7-beat cycle divided as 3 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). To accommodate the fast tempo (drut), each row below shows two consecutive cycles side-by-side: the first seven columns correspond to cycle 1, and the next seven columns correspond to cycle 2. This creates a 14-column layout spanning two complete measures.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Śyāma
text: Composer: Sadāraṅga  ·  Tāl: Ektāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Śyāma
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

papīhā bole le re sāvana kī ritu āi toresadāraṅga upayogī piya gara lāgorīdhāya rasa batiyā barasa barasa re

Antarā

dāmini kuḍhata jīya ḍara lāge na kauna rahungī nyāremahammadaśā pyāre hita deya cita deya nani nani bundariyaprīta sarasa sarasa re








NoteReading the notation





Ektāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, X, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, khālī O at beat 3, tali X (often written as 2) at beat 5, khālī O at beat 7, tali 3 at beat 9, and tali 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak). Capital letters (like \(M\), \(D\)) denote teevra or shuddh notes depending on the specific raga’s convention compared to lowercase letters.

title: Rāg Megh
text: Composer: Tānasen  ·  Tāl: Jhāpatāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Megh
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

prabala dala megha jhuka jhuma yā bhūma paraumaḍa ghanaghora jhaḍi indra lāyo

Antarā

barakhata musaladhāra hota pahara cārakṛṣṇa giridhara gokula ko bacāyo

Sañcārī

bundana dharaṇī dhara sabahi kī rakṣa karapaśū pañchī jala thala ati sukha pāyo

Ābhog

tānasena ke prabhu terī gati adabhūtasurapati adhīna hoya śīṣa navāyo








NoteReading the notation





Jhāpatāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 2 + 3 + 2 + 3 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, tali 2 at beat 3, khālī O at beat 6, and tali 3 at beat 8.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak). Note: The Bishnupuri interpretation is characterized by the use of śuddha gāndhār and śuddha nishād, in contrast to the more commonly employed komal (flat) variants.

title: Rāg Tilak kāmod
text: Composer: Haranāthā  ·  Tāl: Tīvrā (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Tilak kāmod
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

prathama paravaradigāra hi nādako racoomkāra rūpa bhayo nirañjana kara kara vistārakhaṭasura karata trividha vidhāna

Antarā

khaṭarāga kī khaṭatriśa rāgiṇī sunata veda pramāṇaraṭa deva nāga gandharva kinnara aura sura nara janakara vīṇa kara sura sādha vāṇī sahita rāga kī tānanita gāvata śiva śaṅkara haranātha karata bakhāna








NoteReading the notation





Tīvrā tāl is a 7-beat cycle divided as 3 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). In this notation, two consecutive 7-beat cycles are displayed per row, making a 14-column layout (3+2+2 + 3+2+2).
The columns correspond to beats 1–7 of the first cycle and beats 1–7 of the second cycle. The markers indicate sam X, tali 2, and tali 3 for each cycle.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Sarpardā
text: Composer: Adāraṅga  ·  Tāl: Tilwādā (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Sarpardā
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

raṅga rāti mātiyā āve piyābāta kahata itarāta āve piyā

Antarā

ajahuna saha ninda piyā nainā batanāvecāla calata itarāta āve piyā








NoteReading the notation





Tilwādā is a 16-beat cycle divided as 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, tali 2 at beat 5, khālī O at beat 9, and tali 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Kāphī
text: Composer: Tānabarasa  ·  Tāl: Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Kāphī
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

ruma jhuma barakhe āju bādaravā piyā bideśa mori
tharatharāta chatiyā na niśa dina mana bhāve

Antarā

naina na ninda āve dāminī damaka lāga
una bina kala na parata nātha nātha dhāve

Sanchārī

raho na jāta ghari pala china tana dahe mori
āne madana mosana jujha samaya pāve

Ābhog

nikasata nāhi prāṇa ho rahī cita pakhāna
tāpara karata dhyāna tānabarasa gāve








NoteReading the notation





Suraphāktāl is a 10-beat cycle divided as 4 + 2 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, and 3 at beat 7.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

text: Composer: Tānarāj  ·  Tāl: Suraphāktāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Tilak kāmod
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

śambhu hara pada yuga dhyāni bakhāninātharaṅga tori kīratana dina rainagāvata jana samāja

Antarā

jayati tripuranātha dayāla bīracandraguṇigaṇa prati pālaka tu samāna dātākahi nahi ho rāja

Sañcārī

viṣama samara vijayī bhuupāla oura torekala yuga vicana sama tu adhirāja

Ābhog

agaṇita yaśa kīrata vidyā nidhānajaga jana raṭa vāṇī tānarāja guṇaśravaṇa suna ho rāja








NoteReading the notation





Suraphāktāl is a 10-beat cycle. In this layout, it is divided into blocks corresponding to 4 + 2 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the ten columns correspond to beats 1–10, with sam X at beat 1.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Iman
text: Composer: Guṇasen  ·  Tāl: Cautāl (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Iman
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

sapta sura tina grāma ekiśa mūrachana
bāiśa śurati me unañcāśa koṭa tāna sādha nāda

Antarā

ārohī abarohī āsthāyī sañcāri dhūraṇa mūraṇa
ughaṭa pragaṭa tivara bā nikhāda

Sanchārī

kaṇṭhā bharaṇa banāve sāco paṭarī
paṭhāve sura samvāda

Ābhog

sāre sāre gāgā regā gāresā pāmā gāgā regā gāre
sāregāmā pādhā nidhāpā nidhāpā nini dhādhā pāpā
gāgāresā saregāmā pādhā purta surata miṭata vāda








NoteReading the notation





Cautāl is a 12-beat cycle divided as 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 + 2 (vibhāgs marked X, O, 2, O, 3, 4). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the twelve columns correspond to beats 1–12, with sam X at beat 1, O (khālī) at beat 3, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 7, 3 at beat 9, and 4 at beat 11.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).

title: Rāg Iman kalyāṇ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

śyāma ko daraśana nahi pāyomero mana cancala hota unase jaba hi mile sohi dina hi sudina

Antarā

abahi jāta ḍhunḍhe yamunā taṭa kouna bina ghari ghari pala china bhavana ko nahi rahata mana

Lyrics

Sthāyī

uṭhi cale sudina nācata reālī hūn āyī tohe lena

Antarā

lālana terī ārati karatatua sundara naṭanāgara nārīsudina nācata re ālī hūn āyī tohe lena

title: Sangīt Manjarī
text:
Sangīt Manjarī





  Tagore Edition
  
  
    
  
  
  
    Sangīt Manjarī
    By Ramprasanna Banerjee,Dr. Suparno Bhattacharya,Pt. Arijit Mahalanabis
    
    
    
      🛒 View on Amazon
    
  


Preserving India’s musical heritage means balancing deep respect for tradition with thoughtful documentation. Much of Indian classical music has been passed down orally through the guru-shishya system, creating profound, personal learning—but also making the tradition vulnerable to loss without written records. Saving old bandishes is not just archival work; it’s about keeping the voices of past generations alive for today and tomorrow.
Saṅgīt Manjarī is a key step in this endeavor. Compiled by Pt. Ramprasanna Bandyopadhyay of the Bishnupur Gharānā and first published in 1907, it is one of the earliest written collections of a gharānā’s music, drawing on both his father’s teaching and the broader Bishnupur tradition. The collection is remarkable for its range: while Dhrupad is central, it also features Khayāl, Tappā, Thumri, Chaturang, Jhulan, Trivat, and Thillānā—few works from that era showcase such diversity. Later, around 1935, Pt. Ramprasanna’s brother, Pt. Gopeswar Bandyopadhyay, expanded and edited the book, helping it serve as a core resource for musicians and scholars.
Despite its importance, Saṅgīt Manjarī has long been difficult to access. Scanned versions online are in Braj Bhasha, written with Bengali script, making them hard for most modern readers. Guided by my teacher, Guruji Pt. Arijit Mahalanabis—whose deep knowledge and pioneering work on reviving rare musical texts inspired this project—our team created a new edition (now available on Amazon). This version transliterates the original Braj into Roman script, corrects minor errors, and presents the music using Bhatkhande notation widely adopted in Indian classical music. Our goal is to stay true to the original while making it relevant and accessible today.
This website is the companion to that book. Here, you will find a digital selection of compositions from Saṅgīt Manjarī. Rather than simply reproduce the original, our aim is to make these materials practical and inspiring for the next generation.
Because Saṅgīt Manjarī is so extensive, we are releasing material in installments. The first set features compositions that influenced our national poet Viswakavi Rabindranath Tagore, highlighting the Bishnupur Gharānā’s unique impact on his music and, more broadly, on modern Bengali music.
Ultimately, we hope this project will help preserve India’s musical legacy by combining careful editing with digital sharing. We invite others working with different gharānās to join in and help revive more rare musical traditions.

title: Rāg Iman kalyaṅ
text: Composer: Sadāraṅga  ·  Tāl: Jaladtetālā (Madhyalay)

title: Rāg Iman kalyaṅ
text:
Lyrics

Sthāyī

caturaṅga rasa sana gāye ho gāyana guṇī āye
mahammadaśā ke ghara kāja hastī turaṅga sarasa sukha pāve
dāna māna sundara jara śira pāiye

Antarā

sare sare gaga regagare sa papa gaga resa sare gama papa
dhadhapa dhadhapa ninidha ninidhadha papamama gagarega garesa

Sanchārī

nāderder dāni tomderder diyānāre tom tānānatāna
tānānānā nāder der dāni tadāni tadāre tānānānā nānā
dīm tānādīm tānānānā nānānānā nānānānā nānānānā
dhalāṃ dhalāṃ dhalāṃ dhalāṃ

Ābhog

dṛgitoma dṛgitoma dṛmikeṭe dṛmikeṭe thārikeṭe
thārikeṭe tākujhāku tāku jhāku nāga diga diga
thaṃ naṃ naṃ thuṃ diga diga toma ambo ambo amboiyā
tiyā iyā tiya iyā tiyā iyā tiyā iyā tiyā iyā








NoteReading the notation





Jaladtetālā (drut Tintāl) is a 16-beat cycle divided as 4 + 4 + 4 + 4 (vibhāgs marked X, 2, O, 3). Each row below shows one complete cycle: the sixteen columns correspond to beats 1–16, with sam X at beat 1, 2 at beat 5, O (khālī) at beat 9, and 3 at beat 13.
The sargam row gives the notes; the bold text row below it gives the lyrics syllables. A dash - indicates a held note or empty beat. Brackets \(\lb{\cdot}\) denote meend (glide) or grouped notes; superscripts \(\sps{\cdot}\) indicate the starting grace note of a glide; \(\tr{X}\) denotes a note in the upper octave (tār saptak); \(\mn{X}\) denotes a note in the lower octave (mandra saptak).
